headline: The Palm Oil Nation That Cannot Feed Itself
context: Malaysia exports RM90 billion in palm oil annually but imports 60% of its food, including rice, vegetables, and meat.

cards.big: World's second-largest palm oil exporter. Imports 60% of its food.
cards.sub: The trade surplus hides a food vulnerability nobody prices.

cards.big: Rice self-sufficiency: 63%. Target since 12th Malaysia Plan: 75%.
cards.sub: The target has been missed in every plan cycle since 2006.

cards.big: 4.1 million hectares of palm oil. 680,000 hectares of rice paddy.
cards.sub: Land conversion to palm oil reduced food crop acreage by 28% since 1990.

cards.big: BERNAS monopoly controls rice imports and price stabilisation.
cards.sub: One private company holds national food security as a concession.

cards.big: Export revenue is not food security. Ask Sri Lanka.
cards.sub: A supply chain disruption does not care about your trade surplus.

cards.big: Malaysia optimised for export revenue and outsourced food resilience. The calculation works until it does not.
